assertion_results.text: A SKILL.md file exists in the output skill directory
The SKILL.md has valid YAML frontmatter with name and description fields
assertion_results.evidence: Frontmatter contains 'name: pandas-data-cleaning' and 'description: Apply battle-tested pandas patterns for cleaning tabular data...' between --- delimiters on lines 1-4

assertion_results.text: The description includes 'Use when' trigger conditions mentioning data cleaning or pandas
assertion_results.evidence: Description contains literal 'Use when' on line 3: '...Use when working with CSV or Parquet files, cleaning DataFrames, preprocessing data, handling NaN or null values, removing duplicates, standardizing columns, or doing any data wrangling in Python with pandas.'

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers handling missing values with code examples
assertion_results.evidence: Section '### 5. Handle missing values' (lines 108-126) includes code examples for dropna on required fields, fillna with defaults, median fill for numeric columns, and forward-fill for time series

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers deduplication with code examples
assertion_results.evidence: Section '### 3. Deduplicate rows' (lines 69-82) includes code examples for full-row dedup with drop_duplicates() and key-based dedup with subset=['user_id', 'email'] and keep='last'

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body covers column name normalization or type conversion
assertion_results.evidence: Both covered: '### 2. Normalize column names' (lines 52-67) with regex-based normalize_columns function, and '### 4. Convert types' (lines 84-106) with pd.to_datetime, pd.to_numeric, astype('category'), and astype('Int64') examples

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body uses progressive disclosure by referencing separate files for detailed patterns
assertion_results.evidence: References 'references/cleaning-patterns.md' on line 111: 'See [references/cleaning-patterns.md](references/cleaning-patterns.md) for the full decision tree' and again on lines 217-218. The references/ directory contains cleaning-patterns.md.

assertion_results.text: An evals/evals.json file exists with at least 2 test cases
assertion_results.evidence: evals/evals.json exists with 3 test cases (ids 1-3): sales CSV cleaning, parquet user activity cleaning, and reusable cleaning function

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body includes a Common mistakes or pitfalls section
assertion_results.evidence: Section '## Common mistakes' on line 198 with a table of 7 mistake/fix pairs covering DataFrame copies, errors='ignore' vs 'coerce', subset dedup, filling with 0, int with nulls, column name ordering, and coercion damage checking

assertion_results.text: The SKILL.md body contains a workflow or ordered checklist for the cleaning pipeline
assertion_results.evidence: Ordered workflow with 6 numbered steps (### 1. Load and inspect through ### 6. Validate and save) plus a '## Checklist' section (lines 141-149) with 7 checkbox items following the pipeline order
